_comment: Average cost per visit, typical insurance benefit, and typical gap for common extras (general treatment) services — Australia
_note: Service costs and insurance benefits are national estimates — actual fees vary significantly by provider, location, and insurer. Use as indicative values only. Sub-limits vary widely between policies.
source: Australian Dental Association / Private Health Insurance Ombudsman / industry data

dentalCheckup.label: Dental Check-up & Clean

costPerVisit.description: Annual exam, x-rays, and scale & clean — common annual preventive package

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Preventive dental is included from Basic extras onwards

dentalCheckup.notes: Waiting period: typically 2 months for new policies. No-gap dental packages often available at participating dentists.

dentalFilling.label: Dental Filling

costPerVisit.description: Cost per filling — anterior (front) teeth cheaper; posterior (molar) more expensive. Composite resin most common.

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: General dental included from most Basic extras; better benefits from Mid and Comprehensive

typicalGap.description: Gap is often significant — many insurers have fixed benefit tables that don't keep up with fee increases

dentalFilling.typicalFrequency: As needed (average 1–2 per year for adults)
dentalFilling.notes: Fillings share the dental annual sub-limit with check-ups — budget accordingly.

dentalMajor.label: Crown or Bridge

costPerVisit.description: Cost per crown (porcelain on metal or full ceramic). Bridge costs multiply by number of units.

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Major dental typically requires Mid or Comprehensive cover. Basic extras do not cover major dental.

typicalGap.description: Gaps on major dental are the largest of any extras service — insurance covers only 30–50% of typical cost

dentalMajor.typicalFrequency: As needed (typically once per tooth; 10–20 year lifespan)
dentalMajor.notes: 12-month waiting period on major dental for new policies. Most expensive extras category — a single crown can exhaust your annual limit.

optical.label: Glasses or Contact Lenses

costPerVisit.description: Cost of frames + lenses varies widely by brand and prescription complexity. Basic frames from $200; premium brands $600+

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Most extras policies include optical. Benefit is typically a flat annual limit, not per-item.

typicalGap.description: Gap depends on frame/lens choice — buying entry-level frames from a participating provider can eliminate or reduce the gap

optical.notes: Optical benefit often resets every 1–2 years, not annually. Check your policy's optical benefit cycle. OPSM, Specsavers, and Bailey Nelson have no-gap deals with some insurers.

physio.label: Physiotherapy

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Physiotherapy included in most Mid and Comprehensive extras. Limited or excluded in Basic.

typicalGap.description: Gap per visit is typically $30–$90 — benefits often don't keep pace with fee increases

physio.typicalFrequency: 6–12 sessions per course of treatment, 1–3 courses per year

perVisitLimit.description: Most policies pay a fixed amount per visit (e.g., $45/visit) regardless of actual fee

physio.notes: Annual sub-limit for physio is shared with other allied health (chiro, massage) on many policies. Heavy users can exhaust limits quickly.

chiropractic.label: Chiropractic

costPerVisit.description: Standard chiropractic consultation — initial assessments higher

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Chiropractic included in most Mid and Comprehensive extras. Often excluded or very limited in Basic.

chiropractic.typicalFrequency: 6–24 sessions per year depending on condition
chiropractic.notes: Often shares the allied health sub-limit with physio, remedial massage, and podiatry.

psychology.label: Psychology

costPerVisit.description: Standard 50-minute psychology session. Medicare Better Access rebate ($137.05 per session up to 10/year) significantly offsets costs.

medicareRebate.description: Medicare Better Access provides rebates for up to 10 sessions per calendar year with a GP referral

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Psychology cover varies greatly by policy. Some Mid policies exclude it; Comprehensive typically includes it.

afterMedicareAndInsurance.description: Out-of-pocket after both Medicare rebate and insurance benefit

psychology.notes: Medicare Better Access (GP Mental Health Care Plan) provides 10 sessions/year at subsidised rates regardless of PHI. PHI extras can top up beyond the Medicare benefit.

costPerVisit.description: Standard podiatry consultation including nail care, biomechanical assessment, or orthotic fitting

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Podiatry included in most Mid and Comprehensive extras

remedialMassage.label: Remedial Massage

costPerVisit.description: Standard 60-minute remedial massage by a registered therapist

insuranceBenefit.coverageNote: Included in most Comprehensive extras; many Mid policies also include it

remedialMassage.notes: Benefit levels for remedial massage are often lower than physio/chiro.

breakEvenAnalysis._comment: At what annual usage level does extras cover break even vs cost of premium?
breakEvenAnalysis.methodology: Compare annual extras premium cost to total expected insurance benefits. If benefits < premium, extras is not financially rational.
breakEvenAnalysis.insight: The average Australian with extras cover claims approximately $300 in benefits per year — well below the typical $540–$900 annual premium for extras cover. Extras insurance is financially rational only for heavy users (e.g., regular physio, glasses every year, frequent dental work).

breakEvenExamples.scenario: Annual dental check-up + glasses every 2 years
breakEvenExamples.note: Benefits ~$270/yr — below even Basic extras premium ($540/yr)

breakEvenExamples.scenario: Annual dental check-up + 12 physio sessions + glasses
breakEvenExamples.note: Benefits ~$795/yr — justifies Mid extras ($900/yr) for most users

breakEvenExamples.scenario: Annual dental + major dental work + physio + glasses
breakEvenExamples.note: Major dental year — Comprehensive ($1,380/yr) breaks even
